title: T3-H8

{{Quote|It blew up? Huh, I thought I'd taken care of that little cross-wiring problem.|translated=1|Janice Nall, to Revan after T3-H8's destruction|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
'''T3-H8''' was a [[T3-series utility droid]] with [[Gender/Legends|masculine]] programming that was located on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|city world]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]]. The [[Droid/Legends|droid]] was owned by the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] female [[Janice Nall]] and put up for sale for fifty [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] in her [[Upper City]] [[Droids by Janice|droid shop]].<ref name="KOTOR" /> Like other T3-series [[Cleaning droid/Legends|utility droids]], he was manufactured by [[Duwani Mechanical Products/Legends|Duwani Mechanical Products]]<ref name="CG" /> and had a height of 0.96 [[Meter/Legends|meters]].<ref name="NEGtD" /> He had [[Color/Legends|brown]] and yellow plating and blue, yellow, and red [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]]. The droid had a cross-wiring problem, which Nall assumed she had fixed.<ref name="KOTOR" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
In [[3956 BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> the former [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], who [[Amnesia/Legends|lost]] his memories and believed himself to be a [[Republic soldier]], purchased the droid from Nall. Upon leaving her store, the droid [[Death/Legends|exploded]] due to its cross-wiring. Revan returned to Nall and told her of the situation. Nall refunded Revan the fifty credits, wanting to run an honest business and maintain her reputation.<ref name="KOTOR" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|What? I offer you a full refund and you want more? Business is tough enough as it is… you should be glad I'm giving you a refund at all!|translated=1|Janice Nall to Revan in potential dialogue|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
T3-H8 appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', developed by [[BioWare]]. Interactions with him are optional and do not affect quests. The player is not required to purchase the droid. If they choose to purchase him, he will be destroyed in an unavoidable cutscene when the player walks out of the shop. Upon his destruction, the player may return to Nall to get a refund. She initially offers the full fifty credits, but the player has the option of haggling or threatening Nall to increase it to seventy-five. The latter action grants the player points towards the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]].<ref name="KOTOR" />

T3-H8 is referred to as M4-H8 in some parts of the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]].<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref>
